title: Git Rebasing
description: Learn to rebase branches, interactively rewrite history, continue or abort rebases, and replay commits cleanly.

tasks.explanation: git rebase replays commits on top of another branch tip.

tasks.prompt: Interactively rebase the last 3 commits on the current branch.
tasks.explanation: Interactive rebase lets you squash, edit, or drop recent commits.

tasks.prompt: Continue the rebase after resolving conflicts and staging files.
tasks.explanation: git rebase --continue applies the next commit after conflict resolution.

tasks.prompt: Abort the current rebase and return to the pre-rebase state.
tasks.explanation: git rebase --abort cancels the operation and restores the original branch.

tasks.prompt: Skip the current commit during an interactive rebase.
tasks.explanation: git rebase --skip drops the conflicting commit and moves on.

tasks.prompt: Rebase commits from 'feature' onto 'develop' using explicit onto syntax.
tasks.hint: Use git rebase --onto develop main feature.
tasks.explanation: --onto transplants a commit range onto a new base branch.

tasks.prompt: View the reflog to find commits before a rebase.
tasks.explanation: The reflog records where HEAD and branches have pointed.

tasks.prompt: Apply commit 'a1b2c3d' onto the current branch without merging.
tasks.hint: Use git cherry-pick with the commit hash.
tasks.explanation: git cherry-pick replays a single commit on the current branch.
